evidences.content: Historical Market Data We've made Candlestick, Trade, and AggTrade data for all pairs available for download. What Data Is Available We provide data at daily and monthly intervals. evidences.content: - Symbol - The symbol for which the timeseries data refers - Open - This is the opening price of the time period - High - This is the highest price of the time period - Low - This is the lowest price of the time period - Close - This is the closing price of the time period - Volume (Crypto) - This is the volume in the transacted Ccy. Ie. For BTC/USDT, this is in BTC amount - Volume Base Ccy - This is the volume in the base/converted ccy. Ie. For BTC/USDT, this is in USDT amount - Trade Count - This is the unique number of trades for the given time period [...] - Open - This is the opening price of the time period - High - This is the highest price of the time period - Low - This is the lowest price of the time period - Close - This is the closing price of the time period - Volume (Crypto) - This is the volume in the transacted Ccy. evidences.title: 🕯️ BTC/USDT: Historical Monthly Candle Patterns – Green April Incoming? 📈 | Pampa1 on Binance Square
evidences.content: # 🕯️ BTC/USDT: Historical Monthly Candle Patterns – Green April Incoming? 👋 We're diving into a fascinating analysis of Bitcoin's monthly candle close patterns since 2011, looking for clues about potential future price movements! Bitcoin closed March with a red candle, albeit a close call. This red close, however, might set up a better opportunity for a continued push towards a new All-Time High (ATH). \* Bitcoin closed March with a red candle, meeting expectations but narrowly. \* There have been only three instances of a Green January, Red February, and Red March sequence. \* Out of the previous seven red March closes, five were followed by a green April. \* Of those five green Aprils, four were followed by consecutive green monthly closes. \* May and June have a near 50/50 split between green and red closes. \* The recent March candle close was a "Spinning Top," indicating market indecision, possibly due to macro events. \* Similar situations in 2012 led to a green April and subsequent green months. \* Small red March candle bodies have historically led to mixed April results. \* Statistically, April should close green, with a historical record of 8 green to 5 red closes. \*...

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: #### What is ET to UTC Conversion? ET to UTC conversion helps you translate time between Eastern Time and Coordinated Universal Time. ET encompasses both EST (UTC-5) and EDT (UTC-4) used on the U.S. East Coast. UTC is the global time standard that never changes and serves as the reference for all world timezones. The time difference varies between 4-5 hours. #### Time Zone Information Eastern Time (ET): Generic term encompassing EST (winter) and EDT (summer) in New York, Toronto, Montreal, and Miami. Automatically switches between UTC-5 and UTC-4. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC): The global time standard that never changes. Successor to GMT and used worldwide as the reference for all timezones. Also known as "Zulu Time" in aviation. evidences.title: Timestamp Converter
evidences.content: ## Resources ### Unix time - Wikipedia Unix time (POSIX time or Epoch time) is a system for describing instants in time, defined as the number of seconds that have elapsed since 00:00:00 Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), Thursday, 1 January 1970, not counting leap seconds. ### GMT - Wikipedia Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the mean solar time at the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London. Utc was formerly used as the international civil time standard, now superseded in that function by Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). ### ISO 8601 - Wikipedia [...] ### ISO 8601 - Wikipedia ISO 8601 is an international standard covering the exchange of date- and time-related data. It was issued by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and was first published in 1988. The purpose of this standard is to provide an unambiguous and well-defined method of representing dates and times, so as to avoid misinterpretation of numeric representations of dates and times, particularly when data is transferred between countries with different conventions for writing numeric dates and times. ### UTC - Wikipedia [...] This tools use the date-settings from your computer. If the time or timezone s...

evidences.title: ⛔️⛔️⛔️ BTC/USDT shows a significant and sharp price drop, mo | Mr Mian 786 on Binance Square
evidences.content: Title: ⛔️⛔️⛔️ BTC/USDT shows a significant and sharp price drop, mo | Mr Mian 786 on Binance Square 5:30 pm・10 Oct 2025 edited. # ⛔️⛔️⛔️ BTC/USDT shows a significant and sharp price drop, moving from recent highs near $126,000 to the current price around $107,856.16, representing a -11.12% change in 24 hours. The candles clearly illustrate a major sell-off, with the last large red candle breaking below key moving averages (MA(7) and MA(25) are shown on the chart). ​Such rapid price declines in Bitcoin are typically triggered by a combination of factors, which can include:. ​Macroeconomic Concerns: Broad market risk-off sentiment due to high interest rates, inflation worries, or global economic instability can cause investors to liquidate riskier assets like Bitcoin. ​Regulatory Developments: Negative news or rumors regarding stricter cryptocurrency regulation, crackdowns on exchanges, or bans in major jurisdictions often lead to sharp sell-offs. ​Large-Scale Selling (Whales): The sudden sale of a significant amount of Bitcoin by large holders (often termed "whales") can overwhelm market liquidity and instantly drive the price down, triggering cascading liquidations from leverage...

Seconds since Jan 01 1970. (UTC) :: ##### The current epoch translates to | Date | [...] ## What is the unix time stamp? The unix time stamp is a way to track time as a running total of seconds. This count starts at the Unix Epoch on January 1st, 1970 at UTC. Therefore, the unix time stamp is merely the number of seconds between a particular date and the Unix Epoch. It should also be pointed out (thanks to the comments from visitors to this site) that this point in time technically does not change no matter where you are located on the globe. This is very useful to computer systems for tracking and sorting dated information in dynamic and distributed applications both online and clien...

evidences.title: Epoch Converter - Unix Timestamp Converter
evidences.content: ## What is epoch time? The Unix epoch (also called Unix time, POSIX time, or a Unix timestamp) is the number of seconds since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC, not counting leap seconds (ISO 8601: 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z). Strictly speaking, the epoch is Unix time 0, but people often use "epoch" as shorthand for Unix time in general. Some systems store epoch values as signed 32-bit integers, which can break on January 19, 2038, in the Year 2038 Problem. This page converts timestamps in seconds (10 digits), milliseconds (13 digits), and microseconds (16 digits) into readable dates. | Human-readable time | Seconds | --- | | 1 hour | 3600 seconds | | 1 day | 86400 seconds | | 1 week | 604800 seconds | | 1 month (30.44 days) | 2629743 seconds | | 1 year (365.25 days) | 31556926 seconds | [...] Epoch Converter # Epoch & Unix Timestamp Conversion Tools The current Unix epoch time is ## Convert epoch to human-readable date and vice versa Press `c` to clear all forms. ## Epoch dates for the start and end of the year/month/day ## Convert seconds to years, months, days, hours and minutes ## What is epoch time? evidences.content: unexpected results on other timeframes. Ensure the asset has COT data available. Script is mainly focused on commodity futures, such as ES, NQ, YM. It has not been tested against Forex. You will need to define the "Lookback" setting in the script settings. Use the total number of trading days required for your analysis. E.g. if you want a 6 month COT analysis, use the measurement tool to count the quantity of daily candles between now and 6 months ago - use this as your Lookback setting. Adjust as needed for other lookback periods, e.g. 3 months, 12 months etc. Other Info The script provides the ability to customise colours in its settings.
